steps.title: Use the methodological flaw
steps.content: Hartley et al. do not simply disagree with the conclusion of Martinez-Ramos et al.; they identify a possible problem in how the review gathered its evidence. If older studies were unintentionally excluded because they used different terminology, then the evidence base for the conclusion may be incomplete.

steps.title: Reject unsupported alternatives
steps.content: Choice A treats the exclusion as intentional and useful, but the text says it may have been inadvertent. Choice B reverses the problem by claiming the study included research that was too heterogeneous, when the concern is exclusion. Choice C invents a species-count limit and says the conclusion is accurate for included species. Choice D best captures the implication: a methodological limitation may mean the conclusion is not well supported. The answer is D.
